prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `342dd610`.
codex_response:
Rule: every non-`8` singleton moves by a fixed color-coded offset onto a fresh `8` canvas: `1 -> right 1`, `2 -> left 2`, `7 -> up 2`, `9 -> down 2`. I implemented that directly in the verifier at [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_342dd610/verifier.py#L4) and packaged the task in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_342dd610/__init__.py#L1).

The Opus hint was used as the starting hypothesis; the Sonnet hint was discarded because it incorrectly suggested variable move distances for `1` and `2`, which I documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_342dd610/notes.md#L1). The generator at [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_342dd610/generator.py#L4) matches the family by producing square `8`-background grids with sparse, non-adjacent singleton markers in colors `1/2/7/9`, with safe in-bounds placements and mixed single-color / multi-color counts. No new primitive was added.

Validation passed: official verifier `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and the shared build produced [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/342dd610/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/342dd610/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/342dd610/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/342dd610/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/342dd610/previews/preview_03.png). I also sanity-checked the built preview sheet; it shows the expected sparse `8`-background grids with translated singleton markers.
